home.heroBefore: BREAKING: Global catastrophe will destroy the economy within HOURS! Banks are HIDING the truth from you!!
home.heroAfter: The post claims a major economic event is imminent and that banks are withholding information, but provides no specific event, source, or evidence.
home.thesisTitle: Fake news and scam ads run on the same trick.
home.thesisBody: Both use misleading content to manipulate a decision before anyone can verify it. TrueSight treats them as one problem — a single detection engine reads a news article, an advertisement, or a social post the same way, and returns the same kind of clear, calm result every time.
home.step1Body: Paste text, a link, or a screenshot of anything you're about to trust or share.
home.step2Body: TrueSight reads the content and checks it against known manipulation patterns.
home.step3Body: Get a trust score, the specific techniques found, and a calm, neutral rewrite.
home.step4Body: Pass along the facts — or the warning — with real understanding behind it.
home.stat1Body: 0–100, reflecting manipulation risk — never a verdict on truth or falsehood.
home.stat2Body: Sensationalism, pressure tactics, fraud indicators, and more, called out specifically.
home.stat3Body: The same claim, stripped of emotional language, so you can judge it calmly.
home.bilingualNote: Works the same way in Arabic and English — the language is detected automatically, never chosen by hand.

analyze.title: Analyze something
analyze.subtitle: Paste text, a link, or upload a screenshot. TrueSight detects the language and content type on its own.
analyze.placeholderText: Paste the article, ad, or post text here…
analyze.uploadPrompt: Click to upload an image, or drag one in
analyze.errorGeneric: Something went wrong reaching the analysis engine.
analyze.errorConnection: Can't reach the backend at localhost:8000. Is it running?

results.indicatorsEmpty: No manipulation techniques detected in this content.

tips.title: Educational Tips
tips.subtitle: Habits that catch manipulation before a Trust Score ever has to.

items.title: Slow down on urgency
items.body: "Act now," countdown timers, and "before it's too late" framing exist to get you to decide before you can check. Treat urgency itself as a signal to slow down.

items.title: Follow the money or the vote
items.body: Ask who benefits if you believe this and share it. An ad benefits its seller; a political post often benefits someone running for something. That doesn't make it false — but it's a reason to verify.

items.title: Read past the headline
items.body: Headlines are written to be shared, not to be complete. The manipulation often lives in what the headline leaves out.

items.title: Real institutions rarely ask this way
items.body: A bank, ministry, or delivery company won't ask for your password or full card number through a link in an urgent message. That request is the tell, regardless of how official it looks.

items.title: Check who else is reporting it
items.body: A major real event is covered by more than one outlet. If a claim only exists on one page or one account, that's worth noticing.

items.title: Notice your own reaction
items.body: Content designed to manipulate usually aims at a strong feeling first — fear, outrage, or excitement — and reasoning second. Noticing the feeling is the first step to checking the claim.

about.title: About TrueSight AI
about.subtitle: Built for the UNESCO Youth Hackathon 2026, Media and Information Literacy theme.
about.missionBody: Most tools split "fake news" and "scam ads" into separate problems. TrueSight starts from the observation that they're the same problem wearing different clothes: content designed to make you decide before you verify. One analysis engine, one Trust Score, one calm rewrite — for a headline, an ad, or a post.
about.teamBody: A six-person student team covering AI, backend, frontend, the browser extension, and dataset research.
about.techBody: Python and FastAPI on the backend, a Claude-powered analysis engine, a React and Tailwind website, and a Chrome/Edge extension — all reading from the same shared contract, in Arabic and English.

footer.builtFor: Built for the UNESCO Youth Hackathon 2026
